decisionRationale: ADOPTION_FUNNEL_NEXT identifies conversion (interest → first use) as the bottleneck. The #1 recommended fix is a strong first-task guide. Current version was 1792 bytes / 45 lines of vague advice — insufficient to convert a visitor into someone who actually runs the tool. This is not fake progress: it's a structural repo-surface improvement with zero blockers, directly addressing the conversion bottleneck.

actions.before: 1792 bytes, 45 lines of vague task-type categories with no spec template, no execution walkthrough, no review loop, no honest-assessment section
actions.after: 11421 bytes, ~210 lines: full conversion guide with task selection criteria + good/bad tables, spec template + real example, execution command, morning-after review loop, next-steps options, honest assessment (when it works/when it doesn't), links to asciinema V2 demo, START_HERE, prompt templates, blog deep-dive, install/docs
actions.commitSubject: feat(docs): strengthen first-task-guide with full conversion walkthrough
actions.pushStatus: pushed to Codeberg origin + GitHub mirror

optionsRejected.reason: Comparison pages are already decent (~85-96 lines each, consistent CTA footers). SEO tuning them further would be low-marginal-return polishing of an already-adequate surface. The first-task-guide was the bottleneck — visitors land on README, click docs, see a thin guide, and bounce.
ComputingForGeeks contact packet is already current (May 27). Re-surfacing would be fake progress per Principle 3.
Already strengthened in the 10:13 CEST run (asciinema link added, score corrected). Posting window opens tomorrow. Further pre-cooldown touching is fake progress.

optionsRejected.option: Create new agent for social proof
optionsRejected.reason: Principle 10 triggers new agent creation when Codeberg stays flat for 3+ audit runs. While Codeberg is flat at 12 stars, this is a conversion-bottleneck problem, not an awareness-bottleneck problem. A new agent would not fix the conversion surface. Strengthening the first-task-guide is a structural change that directly addresses the funnel bottleneck. Agent creation remains available for next run if Codeberg stays flat.

stateAfterRun.blockedLanes: 7 (unchanged: Reddit, Apollo, GitHub auth, SMTP, PyPI, manual outreach, SO cooldown)
stateAfterRun.activeLanes: none externally; conversion surface is internal-only
stateAfterRun.nextRunWindow: SO posting window opens Wed Jun 3 03:15 CEST (~17h from now). SO answer is prepared, asciinema-linked, and strengthened.

selfImprovementCheck: This run directly executes ADOPTION_FUNNEL_NEXT's highest-priority recommendation (stronger first-task guide). No new self-improvements needed from this run beyond logging the action.
